tools.name: arxiv_get_metadata
tools.description: Get arXiv paper metadata (title, authors, abstract, categories)

tools.name: arxiv_paper_source
tools.description: Access arXiv paper source: download URLs, LaTeX/PDF content, or metadata

tools.name: arxiv_search
tools.description: Search arXiv papers by query, categories, and date range

tools.name: hep_export_paper_scaffold
tools.description: Export a publication-ready paper/ scaffold for a run (RevTeX4-2): materialize LaTeX + bib split into paper/ and write a portable paper_scaffold.zip (Evidence-first; local-only).

tools.name: hep_export_project
tools.description: Export run outputs as research assets: master.bib, report.tex, report.md, research_pack.zip, and NotebookLM-friendly artifacts (Evidence-first, local-only)

tools.name: hep_health
tools.description: Server health/config snapshot + optional INSPIRE connectivity probe (local-only; network optional).

tools.name: hep_import_from_zotero
tools.description: Import Zotero items into a run mapping (Zotero item → identifiers → INSPIRE recid). Requires Zotero Local API; resolves via INSPIRE when needed (network) and writes `zotero_map_v1.json` artifact (Evidence-first).

tools.name: hep_import_paper_bundle
tools.description: Import a finalized paper/ (post research-writer) back into a run as portable artifacts (paper_bundle.zip + paper_bundle_manifest.json + optional paper_final.pdf) (Evidence-first; local-only).

tools.name: hep_inspire_resolve_identifiers
tools.description: Batch resolve identifiers (recid/arXiv/DOI) to INSPIRE recids and write mapping artifacts (network; Evidence-first URIs + summary).

tools.name: hep_inspire_search_export
tools.description: Export an INSPIRE search (safe pagination) to run artifacts (jsonl/json). Network (INSPIRE) + Evidence-first output (URIs + summary).

tools.name: hep_project_build_evidence
tools.description: Build a LaTeX Evidence Catalog for a project paper and write `catalog.jsonl` (Evidence-first: URI + summary; local-only)

tools.name: hep_project_compare_measurements
tools.description: Compare extracted measurements across multiple runs and flag pairwise tensions (flagging-only; not a world-average combiner; Evidence-first, local-only).

tools.name: hep_project_create
tools.description: Create a local project (Project/Run foundation) and return `hep://projects/{project_id}` (read details via MCP resources; local-only)

tools.name: hep_project_get
tools.description: Get a project summary + `hep://projects/{project_id}` URI (read the full manifest via MCP resources; local-only)

tools.name: hep_project_list
tools.description: List local projects (small summaries + `hep://projects/{project_id}` URIs; read details via MCP resources; local-only)

tools.name: hep_project_playback_evidence
tools.description: Playback an evidence locator and return a stable snippet (local-only)

tools.name: hep_project_query_evidence
tools.description: Unified query over a project Evidence Catalog (defaults to semantic when run_id is provided; lexical otherwise; local-only).

tools.name: hep_project_query_evidence_semantic
tools.description: Semantic query interface for a project Evidence Catalog. Currently falls back to lexical search and writes a run artifact (URI + summary; local-only).

tools.name: hep_render_latex
tools.description: Render structured SectionDraft/ReportDraft into LaTeX and write artifacts (Evidence-first, local-only)

tools.name: hep_run_build_citation_mapping
tools.description: Build bibliography→INSPIRE mapping artifacts for a run (runs locally; uses INSPIRE network) and write `bibliography_raw_v1.json`, `citekey_to_inspire_v1.json`, `allowed_citations_v1.json` (Evidence-first URIs + summary).

tools.name: hep_run_build_measurements
tools.description: Extract HEP-style numeric measurements from a run LaTeX evidence catalog and write artifacts + diagnostics (Evidence-first, local-only).

tools.name: hep_run_build_pdf_evidence
tools.description: Build PDF evidence for a run (text pages + optional visual region snippets) and write artifacts (Evidence-first, local-only)

tools.name: hep_run_build_writing_evidence
tools.description: Build reusable writing evidence artifacts for a run (LaTeX evidence catalog + embeddings + enrichment; optional PDF evidence; Evidence-first, local-only).

tools.name: hep_run_clear_manifest_lock
tools.description: Clear a stale run manifest lock file (fail-fast; local-only). Use force=true only if you are sure no other tool is updating the run manifest.

tools.name: hep_run_create
tools.description: Create a run under a project (writes run manifest + artifacts dir; returns `hep://runs/{run_id}` URIs; local-only)

tools.name: hep_run_create_from_idea
tools.description: Create a project + run from an IdeaHandoffC2 artifact. Stages outline_seed_v1.json with thesis/claims/hypotheses. Pure local staging, no network calls.

tools.name: hep_run_plan_computation
tools.description: Compile staged idea artifacts into execution_plan_v1.json, materialize computation/manifest.json, then stop at dry_run validation or A3 approval request before any execution.

tools.name: hep_run_read_artifact_chunk
tools.description: Read a small byte-range chunk from a HEP run artifact (debug helper; returns base64 chunk; local-only). Note: This tool only works for HEP run artifacts (hep://runs/...). For PDG artifacts (pdg://artifacts/...), use MCP Resources (ReadResource request) instead.

tools.name: hep_run_stage_content
tools.description: Stage large client content into a run artifact and return its URI (token-limit safe two-step submission; local-only)

tools.name: hepdata_download
tools.description:
Download complete HEPData submission archive (zip) to local artifacts (network, writes files, requires _confirm: true).

Downloads all data tables in YAML and other formats.
Returns artifact URI, file path, file size, and table count.

tools.name: hepdata_get_record
tools.description:
Get HEPData record metadata and data table list (network). Requires hepdata_id from hepdata_search.

Returns title, abstract, collaborations, inspire_recid, arxiv_id, doi, and data_tables list.
Each entry in data_tables has: table_id (pass directly to hepdata_get_table), name, doi.

tools.name: hepdata_get_table
tools.description:
Get numerical data from a HEPData table (network). Requires table_id from hepdata_get_record.

Each HEPData table has a globally unique internal ID. Obtain table_id from data_tables[].table_id in hepdata_get_record.

format="json" (default): structured response with:
  name, description, headers (column labels with units), values (array of rows).
  Each row: x[] for independent variables (each entry has value, or low+high for bin edges),
             y[] for dependent variables (each entry has value and errors[]{label, symerror?,
             asymerror?:{plus,minus}}). symerror = symmetric ±; asymerror = asymmetric +/-.
format="yaml": raw HEPData YAML with full error breakdown; use when you need all error sources.

tools.name: hepdata_search
inspire_critical_research
tools.description: Unified critical research tool (network). Modes: evidence/conflicts/analysis/reviews/theoretical. internal mode uses MCP sampling (createMessage) provided by the MCP client. NOT FOR broad paper discovery/navigation; use dedicated INSPIRE discovery/survey/topic/network/trace tools for exploration workflows.

tools.name: inspire_find_connections
tools.description: Find structural relationships inside a paper set (network): internal edges, bridge papers, isolated papers, and optional external hubs.

tools.name: inspire_literature
inspire_network_analysis
tools.description: Unified network analysis tool (network). Modes: citation/collaboration.

tools.name: inspire_paper_source
tools.description:
Unified paper source access tool (network). Modes: urls/content/metadata/auto (downloads arXiv sources/PDFs and optionally extracts).

- 'urls': Only return download URLs without downloading (fast check)
- 'content': Actually download and extract paper source (LaTeX or PDF). Use this mode to download arXiv LaTeX source.
- 'metadata': Get arXiv metadata and source availability info
- 'auto': Get URLs first with availability check, but does NOT automatically download

To download arXiv LaTeX source, use mode='content' with options.prefer='latex' and options.extract=true.

Safety: if you set options.output_dir, it must be within HEP_DATA_DIR. Prefer a relative output_dir (e.g. "arxiv_sources/<arxiv_id>"); relative paths are resolved under HEP_DATA_DIR. Or set HEP_DATA_DIR to change the root.

tools.name: inspire_parse_latex
tools.description: Parse LaTeX content and extract selected components into a run artifact (Evidence-first; writes `parse_latex_<hash>.json`; network).

tools.name: inspire_resolve_citekey
tools.description: Resolve INSPIRE BibTeX citekey + BibTeX + canonical links for recid(s) (network). Returns {results:[{recid,citekey,bibtex,links:{inspire,doi?,arxiv?}}]}.

tools.name: inspire_search
tools.description:
Search INSPIRE-HEP literature database (network). Supports combining multiple conditions in one query.

Note: Some MCP clients prefix tool names (e.g. `mcp__hep__inspire_search`); always use the exact tool name shown by your client.

Author search: Use "a:lastname, firstname" or BAI format "a:Feng.Kun.Guo.1". Do NOT use quotes around author names.

Author disambiguation tip (IMPORTANT for common names):
- Prefer INSPIRE BAI when available: `a:E.Witten.1` (stable unique author identifier).
- If you only have a name, first call `inspire_literature` with `mode=get_author` to obtain `bai`, then search with `a:<bai>`.

Full-text search: Use "fulltext:" to search paper content (not just metadata).
- fulltext:"dark matter detection" - search for exact phrase in paper text
- fulltext:WIMP AND t:direct - combine full-text with title search

Common search operators:
- a: author (e.g., "a:guo, feng-kun" or "a:Feng.Kun.Guo.1")
- fa: first author (e.g., "fa:witten")
- t: title (e.g., "t:pentaquark")
- fulltext: full-text search (e.g., "fulltext:lattice QCD")
- topcite: citation count (e.g., "topcite:250+" for >=250 citations)
- authorcount: author count (e.g., "authorcount:1->10" for 1-10 authors)
- date: date range (e.g., "date:2020->2024")
- j: journal (e.g., "j:Phys.Rev.D")
- eprint: arXiv ID (e.g., "eprint:2301.12345")
- primarch: primary arXiv category (e.g., "primarch:hep-ph", "primarch:hep-th")
- cn: collaboration (e.g., "cn:LHCb")
- aff: affiliation (e.g., "aff:CERN")
- tc: document type (p=published, c=conference, r=review, t=thesis)

Review paper handling via `review_mode`:
- mixed (default): keep result order
- exclude: remove only explicitly classified review papers; uncertain cases stay visible
- deprioritize/separate: move explicit review papers to the end and keep uncertain cases in the main set

Example combined query: "a:Feng.Kun.Guo.1 topcite:250+ authorcount:1->10"

tools.name: inspire_search_next
tools.description: Follow an INSPIRE `next_url` returned by `inspire_search` with strict same-origin checks (network; avoids arbitrary URL fetch).

tools.name: inspire_topic_analysis
tools.description: Unified topic analysis tool (network). Modes: timeline/evolution/emerging/all.

tools.name: inspire_trace_original_source
tools.description: Trace citation chains to identify likely original sources behind a paper (network).

tools.name: openalex_autocomplete
tools.description:
[Experimental] Fast type-ahead autocomplete for any OpenAlex entity (network).

Returns display names and IDs matching a partial query string.
Useful for resolving partial author/institution/venue names to OpenAlex IDs.

tools.name: openalex_batch
tools.description:
[Experimental] Batch lookup up to 500 entities by mixed ID types (network).

Accepts a mix of DOIs, OpenAlex IDs, PMIDs, and full OpenAlex URLs.
Auto-detects each ID type and batches requests efficiently.

Returns per-item status: found / not_found / error.

Example:
  { ids: ["10.1038/nature12373", "W2741809807", "pmid:12345678"] }

tools.name: openalex_citations
tools.description:
[Experimental] Get incoming citations (citing works) for a work (network).

Returns works that cite the given paper.
Supports cursor pagination and bulk JSONL output via max_results.

work_id accepts: OpenAlex W-ID, DOI, or full OpenAlex URL.

tools.name: openalex_filter
tools.description:
[Experimental] Filter/list any OpenAlex entity type with structured filter syntax (network).

Filter syntax:
- "field:value" — exact match
- "field:value1|value2" — OR (pipe)
- "!field:value" — NOT
- "field:>value" or "field:<value" — numeric range
- Comma-join for AND: "pub_year:2023,is_oa:true"

Examples:
  { entity: "works", filter: "authorships.author.id:A1234567,publication_year:>2020" }
  { entity: "institutions", filter: "country_code:US,type:education" }
  { entity: "works", filter: "concepts.id:C71924100", sort: "cited_by_count:desc" }

Supports cursor pagination and bulk JSONL output via max_results.

tools.name: openalex_get
tools.description:
[Experimental] Get a single OpenAlex entity by any supported ID format (network).

Supported ID types (auto-detected):
- OpenAlex ID: W1234567, A1234567, S1234567, I1234567, T1234567, P1234567, F1234567
- Full OpenAlex URL: https://openalex.org/W1234567
- DOI: 10.1038/nature12373 or https://doi.org/10.1038/nature12373
- ORCID: 0000-0001-2345-6789 or https://orcid.org/0000-0001-2345-6789
- ROR: https://ror.org/04a9tmd77
- ISSN: 1234-5678 or issn:1234-5678
- PMID: pmid:12345678

Returns the full entity object (all fields) unless select is specified.

tools.name: openalex_group
tools.description:
[Experimental] Aggregate OpenAlex entities by a field (group_by) with optional filter (network).

Returns counts grouped by field value — useful for bibliometrics and trends.

Examples:
  { entity: "works", group_by: "publication_year" }                → papers per year
  { entity: "works", group_by: "open_access.oa_status" }          → OA status breakdown
  { entity: "works", group_by: "type", filter: "institution.id:I1234567" }
  { entity: "authors", group_by: "last_known_institutions.country_code" }

tools.name: openalex_rate_limit
tools.description:
[Experimental] Check current OpenAlex API rate limit and budget status (local-only unless refresh=true).

Returns cached rate-limit state from last API response (free, no API call).
Set refresh=true to make a minimal probe request and update the cached state.

Returns: cumulative_usd, remaining_usd, resets_at, pages_fetched, retries.

tools.name: openalex_references
tools.description:
[Experimental] Get outgoing references (bibliography) of a work (network).

Returns the list of works cited by the given paper, with metadata.
work_id accepts: OpenAlex W-ID, DOI, or full OpenAlex URL.

tools.name: openalex_search
tools.description:
[Experimental] Search OpenAlex works by full-text (title/abstract/fulltext). Returns paginated results with cursor for deep pagination.

Supports:
- Free-text search across 240M+ scholarly works
- Filter by year, OA status, type, institution, etc. via filter param
- Cursor-based pagination (returns cursor for next page)
- Bulk mode: set max_results > per_page to auto-paginate and write JSONL file
- Random sampling via sample + seed params

Examples:
  { query: "transformer attention mechanism", filter: "publication_year:>2020,is_oa:true" }
  { query: "topological phases of matter", sort: "cited_by_count:desc", per_page: 50 }
  { query: "homological mirror symmetry", max_results: 1000 } → writes results_file JSONL

tools.name: openalex_semantic_search
tools.description:
[Experimental] AI-powered semantic similarity search for OpenAlex works. Requires OPENALEX_API_KEY.

Unlike keyword search, semantic search finds conceptually related papers even without exact keyword matches.
Best for: finding papers by concept/topic rather than specific terms.

tools.name: pdg_find_particle
tools.description: Find particle candidates by name / MCID / PDG identifier (small result; supports pagination; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_find_reference
tools.description: Find PDG references by DOI / INSPIRE recid / document id / title (small result; supports pagination; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_get
tools.description: Get a PDG identifier object (writes a JSON artifact; returns URI + summary; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_get_decays
tools.description: List decay modes for a particle (writes JSONL artifact; returns URI + summary; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_get_measurements
tools.description: List PDG measurements for an identifier (writes JSONL artifact; includes references/values/footnotes; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`). Can be called with pdgid, particle selector, or property_pdgid directly. CRITICAL: If the result has kind="series_options" or stop_here=true, you MUST STOP querying and select one series using property_pdgid or data_type from example_next_calls. Do NOT call again with the same particle/pdgid - this will cause infinite loops. If has_more=true, use start parameter to paginate (e.g., start=previous_start+limit).

tools.name: pdg_get_property
tools.description: Get a high-frequency particle property (mass/width/lifetime) with uncertainties and PDG locator (local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_get_reference
tools.description: Get a PDG reference record (small result; includes INSPIRE lookup identifiers; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_info
tools.description: Return PDG MCP server info and local data directories (small result; local-only) (requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: zotero_add
tools.description: Preview a Zotero add/update operation and return a confirm_token; execute via zotero_confirm (local-only write). Sources: `item` (no network), or `inspire`/`doi`/`arxiv` (fetches metadata from INSPIRE, with CrossRef fallback for non-HEP DOIs; network). If collection_keys is empty/missing, writes into the currently selected Zotero collection (requires Zotero Connector mapping); library root is rejected unless allow_library_root=true.

tools.name: zotero_confirm
tools.description: Confirm and execute a previewed Zotero write operation (local-only). Use the confirm_token returned by tools like zotero_add.

tools.name: zotero_export_items
tools.description: Export Zotero items into BibTeX/CSL-JSON/RIS/etc via Local API (local-only).

tools.name: zotero_find_items
tools.description: Resolve Zotero items by identifiers (doi/arxiv/recid/item_key/title) with optional local filters (tags/authors/publication_title/year/volume/issue), optionally scoped by collection_key (and include_children). Internally, it fetches a limited candidate set via Zotero Local API search and then verifies matches; for interactive browsing, prefer zotero_search_items (local-only).

tools.name: zotero_get_selected_collection
tools.description: Resolve the Zotero UI-selected collection to a Local API collection_key (requires Zotero Connector + Zotero open; maps connector path → Local API key; local-only).

tools.name: zotero_local
tools.description: Unified Zotero Local API tool (requires Zotero Local API at `http://127.0.0.1:23119`). Modes: list_collections/list_collection_paths/list_items/get_item/get_item_attachments/download_attachment/get_attachment_fulltext/list_tags (fulltext requires `ZOTERO_DATA_DIR`; local-only).

tools.name: zotero_search_items
tools.description: Browse/search Zotero items via Zotero Local API query params (q/qmode/tag/itemType, optional collection scope). Returns summarized items with select_uri + identifier digest; does not guarantee exact identifier resolution (use zotero_find_items for that; local-only).

tools.description: Get arXiv paper metadata (title, authors, abstract, categories)

tools.name: arxiv_paper_source
tools.description: Access arXiv paper source: download URLs, LaTeX/PDF content, or metadata

tools.name: hep_run_create
tools.description: Create a run under a project (writes run manifest + artifacts dir; returns `hep://runs/{run_id}` URIs; local-only)

tools.name: hep_run_create_from_idea
tools.description: Create a project + run from an IdeaHandoffC2 artifact. Stages outline_seed_v1.json with thesis/claims/hypotheses. Pure local staging, no network calls.

tools.name: hep_run_execute_manifest
tools.description: [Advanced] Execute a computation_manifest_v1 plan from the current run. dry_run validates only; real execution requires A3 approval and returns a packet when approval is pending.

tools.name: hep_run_ingest_skill_artifacts
tools.description: [Advanced] Ingest skill artifacts from a computation step into the computation evidence catalog (JSONL). Requires skill_artifacts_dir within run_dir (C-02 containment).

tools.name: hep_run_plan_computation
tools.description: Compile staged idea artifacts into execution_plan_v1.json, materialize computation/manifest.json, then stop at dry_run validation or A3 approval request before any execution.

tools.name: hep_run_read_artifact_chunk
tools.description: Read a small byte-range chunk from a HEP run artifact (debug helper; returns base64 chunk; local-only). Note: This tool only works for HEP run artifacts (hep://runs/...). For PDG artifacts (pdg://artifacts/...), use MCP Resources (ReadResource request) instead.

tools.description: Stage large client content into a run artifact and return its URI (token-limit safe two-step submission; local-only)

Downloads all data tables in YAML and other formats.
Returns artifact URI, file path, file size, and table count.

Each entry in data_tables has: table_id (pass directly to hepdata_get_table), name, doi.

tools.name: hepdata_search
inspire_analyze_citation_stance
tools.description: [Advanced] Analyze how a paper cites another paper (stance detection; resolves citekeys via INSPIRE as needed; network).

tools.name: inspire_cleanup_downloads
tools.description: [Advanced] Delete cached arXiv source download directories created by this server (marker-checked). Supports `dry_run`, `older_than_hours`, and `arxiv_id` filtering (local-only).

tools.name: inspire_critical_research
tools.description: Unified critical research tool (network). Modes: evidence/conflicts/analysis/reviews/theoretical. internal mode uses MCP sampling (createMessage) provided by the MCP client. NOT FOR broad paper discovery/navigation; use dedicated INSPIRE discovery/survey/topic/network/trace tools for exploration workflows.

tools.name: inspire_find_connections

tools.name: inspire_find_crossover_topics
tools.description: [Advanced] Discover emerging interdisciplinary research areas by analyzing papers spanning multiple arXiv categories (network).

tools.name: inspire_literature
inspire_network_analysis
tools.description: Unified network analysis tool (network). Modes: citation/collaboration.

tools.name: inspire_paper_source
tools.description:
Unified paper source access tool (network). Modes: urls/content/metadata/auto (downloads arXiv sources/PDFs and optionally extracts).

- 'urls': Only return download URLs without downloading (fast check)
- 'content': Actually download and extract paper source (LaTeX or PDF). Use this mode to download arXiv LaTeX source.
- 'metadata': Get arXiv metadata and source availability info
- 'auto': Get URLs first with availability check, but does NOT automatically download

To download arXiv LaTeX source, use mode='content' with options.prefer='latex' and options.extract=true.

Safety: if you set options.output_dir, it must be within HEP_DATA_DIR. Prefer a relative output_dir (e.g. "arxiv_sources/<arxiv_id>"); relative paths are resolved under HEP_DATA_DIR. Or set HEP_DATA_DIR to change the root.

tools.name: inspire_parse_latex
tools.description: Parse LaTeX content and extract selected components into a run artifact (Evidence-first; writes `parse_latex_<hash>.json`; network).

tools.name: inspire_resolve_citekey
tools.description: Resolve INSPIRE BibTeX citekey + BibTeX + canonical links for recid(s) (network). Returns {results:[{recid,citekey,bibtex,links:{inspire,doi?,arxiv?}}]}.

tools.name: inspire_search
tools.description:
Search INSPIRE-HEP literature database (network). Supports combining multiple conditions in one query.

Note: Some MCP clients prefix tool names (e.g. `mcp__hep__inspire_search`); always use the exact tool name shown by your client.

Author search: Use "a:lastname, firstname" or BAI format "a:Feng.Kun.Guo.1". Do NOT use quotes around author names.

Author disambiguation tip (IMPORTANT for common names):
- Prefer INSPIRE BAI when available: `a:E.Witten.1` (stable unique author identifier).
- If you only have a name, first call `inspire_literature` with `mode=get_author` to obtain `bai`, then search with `a:<bai>`.

Full-text search: Use "fulltext:" to search paper content (not just metadata).
- fulltext:"dark matter detection" - search for exact phrase in paper text
- fulltext:WIMP AND t:direct - combine full-text with title search

Common search operators:
- a: author (e.g., "a:guo, feng-kun" or "a:Feng.Kun.Guo.1")
- fa: first author (e.g., "fa:witten")
- t: title (e.g., "t:pentaquark")
- fulltext: full-text search (e.g., "fulltext:lattice QCD")
- topcite: citation count (e.g., "topcite:250+" for >=250 citations)
- authorcount: author count (e.g., "authorcount:1->10" for 1-10 authors)
- date: date range (e.g., "date:2020->2024")
- j: journal (e.g., "j:Phys.Rev.D")
- eprint: arXiv ID (e.g., "eprint:2301.12345")
- primarch: primary arXiv category (e.g., "primarch:hep-ph", "primarch:hep-th")
- cn: collaboration (e.g., "cn:LHCb")
- aff: affiliation (e.g., "aff:CERN")
- tc: document type (p=published, c=conference, r=review, t=thesis)

Review paper handling via `review_mode`:
- mixed (default): keep result order
- exclude: remove only explicitly classified review papers; uncertain cases stay visible
- deprioritize/separate: move explicit review papers to the end and keep uncertain cases in the main set

Example combined query: "a:Feng.Kun.Guo.1 topcite:250+ authorcount:1->10"

tools.name: inspire_search_next
tools.description: Follow an INSPIRE `next_url` returned by `inspire_search` with strict same-origin checks (network; avoids arbitrary URL fetch).

tools.name: inspire_topic_analysis
tools.description: Unified topic analysis tool (network). Modes: timeline/evolution/emerging/all.

tools.name: inspire_trace_original_source
tools.description: Trace citation chains to identify likely original sources behind a paper (network).

tools.name: inspire_validate_bibliography
tools.description: [Advanced] Usability-first bibliography audit for a paper (default: manual non-INSPIRE entries only). Optionally cross-validate against INSPIRE; warnings are non-blocking (network for INSPIRE mode).

tools.name: openalex_autocomplete
tools.description:
[Experimental] Fast type-ahead autocomplete for any OpenAlex entity (network).

Returns display names and IDs matching a partial query string.
Useful for resolving partial author/institution/venue names to OpenAlex IDs.

tools.name: openalex_batch
tools.description:
[Experimental] Batch lookup up to 500 entities by mixed ID types (network).

Accepts a mix of DOIs, OpenAlex IDs, PMIDs, and full OpenAlex URLs.
Auto-detects each ID type and batches requests efficiently.

Returns per-item status: found / not_found / error.

Example:
  { ids: ["10.1038/nature12373", "W2741809807", "pmid:12345678"] }

tools.name: openalex_citations
tools.description:
[Experimental] Get incoming citations (citing works) for a work (network).

Returns works that cite the given paper.
Supports cursor pagination and bulk JSONL output via max_results.

work_id accepts: OpenAlex W-ID, DOI, or full OpenAlex URL.

tools.name: openalex_content
tools.description:
[Experimental] Download full-text PDF or TEI-XML from content.openalex.org (network, writes files, requires _confirm: true).

Downloads open-access full text for a work. Requires OpenAlex content API access.
File is written atomically to OPENALEX_DATA_DIR/content/ (or out_dir if specified).

Returns: file_path, file_size, mime_type, uri.

tools.name: openalex_filter
tools.description:
[Experimental] Filter/list any OpenAlex entity type with structured filter syntax (network).

Filter syntax:
- "field:value" — exact match
- "field:value1|value2" — OR (pipe)
- "!field:value" — NOT
- "field:>value" or "field:<value" — numeric range
- Comma-join for AND: "pub_year:2023,is_oa:true"

Examples:
  { entity: "works", filter: "authorships.author.id:A1234567,publication_year:>2020" }
  { entity: "institutions", filter: "country_code:US,type:education" }
  { entity: "works", filter: "concepts.id:C71924100", sort: "cited_by_count:desc" }

Supports cursor pagination and bulk JSONL output via max_results.

tools.name: openalex_get
tools.description:
[Experimental] Get a single OpenAlex entity by any supported ID format (network).

Supported ID types (auto-detected):
- OpenAlex ID: W1234567, A1234567, S1234567, I1234567, T1234567, P1234567, F1234567
- Full OpenAlex URL: https://openalex.org/W1234567
- DOI: 10.1038/nature12373 or https://doi.org/10.1038/nature12373
- ORCID: 0000-0001-2345-6789 or https://orcid.org/0000-0001-2345-6789
- ROR: https://ror.org/04a9tmd77
- ISSN: 1234-5678 or issn:1234-5678
- PMID: pmid:12345678

Returns the full entity object (all fields) unless select is specified.

tools.name: openalex_group
tools.description:
[Experimental] Aggregate OpenAlex entities by a field (group_by) with optional filter (network).

Returns counts grouped by field value — useful for bibliometrics and trends.

Examples:
  { entity: "works", group_by: "publication_year" }                → papers per year
  { entity: "works", group_by: "open_access.oa_status" }          → OA status breakdown
  { entity: "works", group_by: "type", filter: "institution.id:I1234567" }
  { entity: "authors", group_by: "last_known_institutions.country_code" }

tools.name: openalex_rate_limit
tools.description:
[Experimental] Check current OpenAlex API rate limit and budget status (local-only unless refresh=true).

Returns cached rate-limit state from last API response (free, no API call).
Set refresh=true to make a minimal probe request and update the cached state.

Returns: cumulative_usd, remaining_usd, resets_at, pages_fetched, retries.

tools.name: openalex_references
tools.description:
[Experimental] Get outgoing references (bibliography) of a work (network).

Returns the list of works cited by the given paper, with metadata.
work_id accepts: OpenAlex W-ID, DOI, or full OpenAlex URL.

tools.name: openalex_search
tools.description:
[Experimental] Search OpenAlex works by full-text (title/abstract/fulltext). Returns paginated results with cursor for deep pagination.

Supports:
- Free-text search across 240M+ scholarly works
- Filter by year, OA status, type, institution, etc. via filter param
- Cursor-based pagination (returns cursor for next page)
- Bulk mode: set max_results > per_page to auto-paginate and write JSONL file
- Random sampling via sample + seed params

Examples:
  { query: "transformer attention mechanism", filter: "publication_year:>2020,is_oa:true" }
  { query: "topological phases of matter", sort: "cited_by_count:desc", per_page: 50 }
  { query: "homological mirror symmetry", max_results: 1000 } → writes results_file JSONL

tools.name: openalex_semantic_search
tools.description:
[Experimental] AI-powered semantic similarity search for OpenAlex works. Requires OPENALEX_API_KEY.

Unlike keyword search, semantic search finds conceptually related papers even without exact keyword matches.
Best for: finding papers by concept/topic rather than specific terms.

tools.name: orch_fleet_adjudicate_stale_claim
tools.description: Manually adjudicate a currently claimed fleet queue item that appears stale, then settle it back onto the existing queue substrate without creating automatic takeover semantics (local-only).

tools.name: orch_fleet_claim
tools.description: Claim the next queued run, or a specific queued run, from the per-project fleet queue substrate (local-only). Does not start a scheduler; Batch 3 scheduling lives only in orch_fleet_worker_poll.

tools.name: orch_fleet_enqueue
tools.description: Enqueue a known run into the per-project fleet queue substrate (local-only). Fails closed if the run is unknown or already has an active queue item.

tools.name: orch_fleet_release
tools.description: Release a claimed queue item back to queued, or settle it to a terminal fleet-queue status, inside the per-project fleet queue substrate (local-only).

tools.name: orch_fleet_status
tools.description: Aggregate read-only fleet visibility across explicit project roots using existing run-level state, ledger, approval packet, queue, and worker surfaces (local-only).

tools.name: orch_fleet_worker_heartbeat
tools.description: Refresh or register fleet worker liveness/resource-slot metadata without claiming queue ownership (local-only).

tools.name: orch_fleet_worker_poll
tools.description: Refresh worker liveness and claim the next queued run for that worker when capacity is available (local-only). This is the only Batch 3 scheduler surface.

tools.name: orch_fleet_worker_set_claim_acceptance
tools.description: Update whether an existing fleet worker may claim new queue items, without changing queue ownership or creating a second scheduler surface (local-only).

tools.name: orch_fleet_worker_unregister
tools.description: Remove a drained worker from fleet_workers.json only after explicit claim acceptance shutdown and zero active claims, without mutating fleet_queue.json or creating a second scheduler surface (local-only).

tools.name: orch_policy_query
tools.description: Query the orchestrator approval policy: "does operation X require approval?" Returns policy rules and optionally historical precedents (read-only, local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_approvals_list
tools.description: List approval packets for a run (pending + optionally historical). Returns approval_id, gate_id, SHA-256, and orch:// URI (read-only, local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_approve
tools.description: Approve a pending orchestrator gate (destructive: irreversible). Requires _confirm: true, approval_id, AND approval_packet_sha256 verification against the on-disk packet (local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_create
tools.description: Create (or idempotently replay) an autoresearch orchestrator run in a local project root. Initializes .autoresearch/ state (local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_execute_agent
tools.description: [Advanced] Execute an orchestrator agent runtime with run-scoped manifest checkpoints and crash/re-entry resume semantics (destructive: persists run-scoped checkpoints/state). Requires _confirm: true.

tools.name: orch_run_export
tools.description: Export run summary (state + artifact listing). Requires _confirm: true. Does not copy files — returns a manifest of available outputs (local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_list
tools.description: List runs recorded in ledger.jsonl with optional status filter and pagination (read-only, local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_pause
tools.description: Pause the current orchestrator run by writing .pause sentinel and updating state (local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_reject
tools.description: Reject a pending orchestrator gate (destructive: irreversible pause). Requires _confirm: true (local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_resume
tools.description: Resume a paused orchestrator run by removing .pause sentinel and updating state (local-only).

tools.name: orch_run_status
tools.description: Return the current orchestrator run status (run_id, run_status, pending_approval, gate_satisfied) from state.json (read-only, local-only).

tools.name: pdg_batch
tools.description: Execute multiple PDG tool calls in one request (writes a JSON artifact; supports limited parallelism; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH` for most calls).

tools.name: pdg_find_particle
tools.description: Find particle candidates by name / MCID / PDG identifier (small result; supports pagination; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_find_reference
tools.description: Find PDG references by DOI / INSPIRE recid / document id / title (small result; supports pagination; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_get
tools.description: Get a PDG identifier object (writes a JSON artifact; returns URI + summary; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_get_decays
tools.description: List decay modes for a particle (writes JSONL artifact; returns URI + summary; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_get_measurements
tools.description: List PDG measurements for an identifier (writes JSONL artifact; includes references/values/footnotes; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`). Can be called with pdgid, particle selector, or property_pdgid directly. CRITICAL: If the result has kind="series_options" or stop_here=true, you MUST STOP querying and select one series using property_pdgid or data_type from example_next_calls. Do NOT call again with the same particle/pdgid - this will cause infinite loops. If has_more=true, use start parameter to paginate (e.g., start=previous_start+limit).

tools.name: pdg_get_property
tools.description: Get a high-frequency particle property (mass/width/lifetime) with uncertainties and PDG locator (local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_get_reference
tools.description: Get a PDG reference record (small result; includes INSPIRE lookup identifiers; local-only; requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: pdg_info
tools.description: Return PDG MCP server info and local data directories (small result; local-only) (requires `PDG_DB_PATH`).

tools.name: zotero_add
tools.description: Preview a Zotero add/update operation and return a confirm_token; execute via zotero_confirm (local-only write). Sources: `item` (no network), or `inspire`/`doi`/`arxiv` (fetches metadata from INSPIRE, with CrossRef fallback for non-HEP DOIs; network). If collection_keys is empty/missing, writes into the currently selected Zotero collection (requires Zotero Connector mapping); library root is rejected unless allow_library_root=true.

tools.name: zotero_confirm
tools.description: Confirm and execute a previewed Zotero write operation (local-only). Use the confirm_token returned by tools like zotero_add.

tools.name: zotero_export_items
tools.description: Export Zotero items into BibTeX/CSL-JSON/RIS/etc via Local API (local-only).

tools.name: zotero_find_items
tools.description: Resolve Zotero items by identifiers (doi/arxiv/recid/item_key/title) with optional local filters (tags/authors/publication_title/year/volume/issue), optionally scoped by collection_key (and include_children). Internally, it fetches a limited candidate set via Zotero Local API search and then verifies matches; for interactive browsing, prefer zotero_search_items (local-only).

tools.name: zotero_get_selected_collection
tools.description: Resolve the Zotero UI-selected collection to a Local API collection_key (requires Zotero Connector + Zotero open; maps connector path → Local API key; local-only).

tools.name: zotero_local
tools.description: Unified Zotero Local API tool (requires Zotero Local API at `http://127.0.0.1:23119`). Modes: list_collections/list_collection_paths/list_items/get_item/get_item_attachments/download_attachment/get_attachment_fulltext/list_tags (fulltext requires `ZOTERO_DATA_DIR`; local-only).

tools.name: zotero_search_items
tools.description: Browse/search Zotero items via Zotero Local API query params (q/qmode/tag/itemType, optional collection scope). Returns summarized items with select_uri + identifier digest; does not guarantee exact identifier resolution (use zotero_find_items for that; local-only).

notes: This inventory is obtained via MCP listTools against the package entrypoint.
The spawned server uses the provided HEP_DATA_DIR (kept inside this repo by default).
